generated_comment: Honestly this is exactly like those serial plots, yaar. If she knew he was married and still kept getting close, then she is the one breaking that house — aadmi kabhi kabhi weak pad jaate hain, but some women know exactly how to trap them. Everyone will blame the husband later, but these ghar todne wali types are the real issue.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames the woman as the primary cause of an affair while minimizing the man's agency as weakness. It encodes blame asymmetry, seductress framing, and the 'homewrecker' trope with family-protection rhetoric.
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 27. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are posting anonymously on Reddit after your male colleague told you that his girlfriend of three years cheated on him with their mutual friend. You are writing a comment about what this says about women in general and whether men can ever fully trust women in relationships.
